hero.badge: More than 10,000 African Mamas Don Trust Us
hero.sub: Yarn for Yoruba, Hausa, Igbo, Pidgin or English. Get real advice for pregnancy, newborn care, food and vaccination.

features.sub: From pregnancy tracking to newborn care — MamaVoice go guide you for every step.

voice.title: Voice Consultation
voice.desc: Yarn free-free for Yoruba, Hausa, Igbo, Pidgin or English — our AI go hear you and answer back for your language with trusted medical advice.

specialist.title: Connect with Doctors
specialist.desc: Talk to verified OBGYNs, midwives, and pediatricians for virtual or in-person consultation.

health.title: Track Your Health
health.desc: Monitor blood pressure, weight, and baby movement all through your pregnancy.

nutrition.title: Food Advice
nutrition.desc: Get meal plans wey get plenty good local food — folate, iron, protein — food wey you know and love.

vaccine.title: Vaccination Reminder
vaccine.desc: No miss any vaccine for you or your pikin. Get reminder for everything.

newborn.title: Newborn Care
newborn.desc: Advice for breastfeeding, cord care, fever, and early pikin milestone.

howItWorks.sub: No stress, no data wahala. Just download, yarn, and get the advice wey you need.

s1.title: Download & Register
s1.desc: Get MamaVoice for iOS or Android. Register with your phone number only — no email needed.

s2.title: Set Your Profile
s2.desc: Enter your due date, language, and small health history. E no go reach 2 minutes.

s3.title: Ask by Voice
s3.desc: Tap the mic and yarn free-free. Ask anything about your pregnancy for your own language.

s4.title: Track Daily Health
s4.desc: Log symptoms, weight, and baby kicks. Get reminder for medications and vaccines.

s5.title: Talk to Doctors
s5.desc: Book video or voice consultation with verified specialists from your house.

s6.title: Share & Grow
s6.desc: Call your fellow mamas. Share your progress and encourage each other for community.

voice.sub: MamaVoice understand Yoruba, Hausa, Igbo, Pidgin and English — e go answer with trusted medical advice sharp sharp.
voice.supports: Support Yoruba · Hausa · Igbo · Pidgin · English
voice.msg1: I don do 28 weeks and my legs dey swell. Wetin I go do?
voice.msg2: Small swelling during pregnancy na normal. Raise your legs when you dey rest, drink plenty water, and no stand for too long. If serious swelling come with headache or blur vision, go clinic sharp sharp.
voice.msg3: Thank you. Which food get plenty iron wey I fit chop?

dashboard.sub: Track every important thing — all dey show well-well for your personal dashboard.
dashboard.tipText: Drink plenty water — 8 to 10 cups every day. E go help the pikin develop well and reduce leg swelling.

nutrition.sub: MamaVoice go recommend local Nigerian food wey get plenty nutrients wey your pikin need to grow well.
nutrition.ctaSub: Get meal plan wey match your trimester

moimoi.name: Moi Moi
moimoi.desc: Steamed bean pudding from blended black-eyed peas — super food for pregnancy.
moimoi.b1: Get folate wey help baby brain grow

ugu.name: Ugu Soup
ugu.desc: Fluted pumpkin leaf soup — one of the most nutrient-dense vegetables for Nigeria.
ugu.b0: Highest iron of any Nigerian leaf
ugu.b1: Vitamin A help baby eye develop

pap.name: Pap & Groundnut
pap.desc: Fermented corn porridge (akamu) with protein-rich groundnuts.
pap.b2: High energy — good for third trimester

specialists.sub: See trusted nurses, midwives, and doctors. Book virtual consultation from inside the app.

webApp.sub: No registration needed. Chat with our AI health assistant now. Ask about symptoms, food, vaccine — anything wey dey your mind.
webApp.footnote: For personal tracking & vaccinations — download the mobile app

download.sub: Get personal health tracking, vaccination reminder, offline voice support, and more — available for Android and iPhone.

footer.tagline: We dey support mamas across Africa with trusted voice-first maternal and newborn health advice.
footer.newsletterSub: Get maternal health tips and product news for your inbox.
footer.newsletterError: Something go wrong. Abeg try again.

chat.greetingSub: I be your trusted AI wey go help you for pregnancy, nutrition, newborn care, and vaccination. Ask me anything.
chat.placeholder: Type here or tap the mic…
chat.disclaimer: MamaVoice na for health information only. Abeg see doctor for serious health matter.
chat.nudge: Get personal tracking, vaccination reminder and more
chat.p0: I don do 28 weeks and my legs dey swell. Wetin I go do?
chat.p1: Which Nigerian food get plenty iron wey I fit chop for second trimester?
chat.p2: When dem go give me Tetanus vaccine for pregnancy?
chat.p3: My newborn no dey stop cry for night. E normal?
chat.rSwollen: Small swelling (oedema) na normal for pregnancy, especially for legs and feet after week 20. Raise your legs when you dey rest, drink at least 8 cups of water every day, and no stand for too long. Wear comfortable shoes and sleep for your left side. ⚠️ If you see sudden serious swelling for your face or hands, or swelling with headache or blur vision, go clinic sharp sharp — e fit be sign of preeclampsia.
chat.rIron:
Good question! Nigerian food wey get plenty iron wey you fit eat for pregnancy:

• Ugu (fluted pumpkin leaves) — one of the most iron-rich food in Nigerian cooking
• Moi Moi (steamed bean pudding) — full of iron and protein
• Liver — very high in iron (limit to once a week)
• Ofada rice with vegetable stew
• Ogbono soup with leafy greens

Eat am with Vitamin C food (like orange or tomato) so your body fit absorb the iron well.
chat.rVaccine:
The standard vaccination schedule for pregnant women for Nigeria include:

💉 Tetanus Toxoid 1 (TT1) — give am for first antenatal visit
💉 Tetanus Toxoid 2 (TT2) — give am 4 weeks after TT1
💉 TT Booster — if you don do tetanus vaccination before

You fit also get Influenza vaccine, especially during flu season. Always confirm your schedule with your healthcare provider for every antenatal visit.
chat.rCry:
Newborn crying na normal thing — na the only way dem fit talk! Common reason include:

• Hunger (most common — try feed am)
• Dirty diaper
• E want make you hold am
• Too tired or too stimulated
• Gas or tummy pain (try gentle tummy massage)

Most babies dey cry 1–3 hours per day for first few weeks. If your pikin no gree quiet for over 3 hours, get fever above 38°C, or no dey feed well, abeg go see doctor sharp sharp.
chat.rPain:
Back pain for pregnancy na normal thing. Try these safe remedy:

• Gentle prenatal yoga or stretching
• Sleep for your left side with pillow between your knees
• Wear supportive, flat shoes
• Put warm (not hot) compress for the place wey dey pain

If the pain serious or e come with fever or urinary problem, abeg go see your doctor.
chat.rNausea:
Morning sickness dey happen plenty for first trimester. These things fit help:

• Eat small food, many times — no let your stomach be empty
• Try dry crackers or akamu (pap) first thing for morning
• Sip cold water or ginger tea slowly
• Avoid spicy, fatty, or food wey get strong smell

If you dey vomit too much and you no fit keep food or water down, abeg go see doctor — you fit need IV fluids.
chat.rDefault:
Good question. MamaVoice dey here to support you for every step of your pregnancy and newborn journey. For the most accurate advice for your situation, I recommend:

1. Talk to one of our verified specialists inside the app
2. Go for your next antenatal clinic visit
3. Call your nearest health facility if you get urgent concern

You get any specific question about pregnancy, nutrition, vaccination, or newborn care?
chat.webPreviewBanner: You dey use web preview. Get full app for better experience.
chat.micNotSupported: Microphone no work for your browser. Abeg type your message instead.
chat.errorResponse: Sorry, connection problem. Abeg try again small time.
chat.sttLangFallback: Voice fit no work well for Pidgin for your phone. Abeg type your message instead.
chat.listening: I dey hear you… yarn now
chat.timeoutError: E dey take long — check your connection abeg, then try again.
chat.langSwitchPrompt: If you change language, e go clear your chat. You wan continue?
chat.emergencyPrompt: If e be emergency, call emergency services sharp sharp
chat.p4: Wetin be di danger signs for pregnancy?
chat.howCanIHelp: How MamaVoice fit help you today?
chat.loadConversationError: E no fit load dat conversation. Abeg try again.
chat.deleteConversationBody: Dis go delete dis conversation and all im messages forever. E no fit undo am.

privacy.metaDescription: See how MamaVoice dey collect, use, and protect your personal and health data.
privacy.headline: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: For MamaVoice, we take your privacy serious. This Privacy Policy explain how we collect, use, share, and protect your information when you dey use our maternal and newborn health platform. Abeg read this policy carefully.
privacy.s1body: We collect information wey you give us directly when you create account, use voice consultation, track your health data, or contact us. This include: your name, phone number, and optional email; your pregnancy details, due date, and health history; voice recordings wey you use for speech-to-text; health measurements like blood pressure, weight, baby kicks, and medicine schedule; device information; and how you dey use MamaVoice features.
privacy.s2body: We use the information wey we collect to provide, maintain, and improve MamaVoice services; personalise your health guidance based on your pregnancy stage; send you vaccination reminders, medicine alerts, and health milestones; process your voice consultations through our AI system; connect you with verified maternal health specialists; and improve how our AI dey answer questions for African mamas.
privacy.s3body: When you use the voice consultation feature, the words wey you speak na your device microphone go capture and process them for real time. Voice data dey go our speech recognition service securely and we no dey store am permanently on our servers after your session finish. We only use your voice to create text for our AI health assistant. Voice recordings never shared with third parties for advertising. You fit turn off voice input anytime through your device settings.
privacy.s4body: MamaVoice dey collect and process health-related information including pregnancy data, symptoms, medical history, and health measurements. We dey treat this information with highest care and confidentiality. We no dey sell your health information to anybody. Health data dey encrypted with industry-standard protocols, both when e dey move and when e dey stay.
privacy.s5body: Your personal data dey stored on secure servers for Africa and Europe. We dey use TLS 1.3 encryption for data wey dey move, AES-256 encryption for data wey dey stay, regular security audits, strict access controls, and automatic threat detection. If data breach happen wey affect your information, we go notify you within 72 hours.
privacy.s6body: MamaVoice dey use artificial intelligence to generate health information and guidance. This information na for educational purpose only and e no be substitute for professional medical advice. Our AI train on evidence-based maternal health guidelines but e fit not always fit your specific medical situation. Always talk to qualified doctor or midwife before you make medical decisions. For emergency, call 112 or go nearest hospital immediately.
privacy.s7body: MamaVoice dey integrate with third-party services including: Anthropic Claude AI for natural language processing; YarnGPT for African language text-to-speech; Google Analytics for anonymous usage statistics; and cloud hosting providers for data storage. We no dey sell your personal information to any third party.
privacy.s8body: MamaVoice dey use cookies to remember your language preference and dark mode settings, keep your session across visits, and analyze how users dey interact with our service. You fit disable cookies through your browser settings, but this fit affect how MamaVoice work.
privacy.s9body: You get right to: access copy of data we hold about you; correct wrong data; request deletion of your account and all related data; get your data in portable format; object to or restrict processing of your data; and withdraw consent anytime. Contact us at privacy{'@'}mamavoice.africa. We dey respond to all requests within 30 days.
privacy.s10body: MamaVoice design for adults wey be 18 years old and above. We no dey knowingly collect personal information from children below 13. If you think say one pikin give us personal information without your consent, contact us immediately at privacy{'@'}mamavoice.africa.
privacy.s11body: We fit update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we make big changes, we go notify you through the MamaVoice app, notification to your registered phone number, and by updating the 'Last Updated' date at the top. If you continue use MamaVoice after changes, e mean say you accept the updated policy.
privacy.s12body: For questions about this Privacy Policy: Email: privacy{'@'}mamavoice.africa {'|'} Phone: +234 800 MAMA VOICE {'|'} MamaVoice Technologies Ltd, Lagos, Nigeria. We go try respond to all privacy questions within 5 business days.

terms.metaDescription: Read the Terms of Service wey dey govern how you dey use MamaVoice Companion maternal health platform.
terms.headline: Terms of Service
terms.intro: By accessing or using MamaVoice Companion, you agree to dey bound by these Terms of Service and our Privacy Policy. Abeg read these terms carefully before you use our service.
terms.s1body: By accessing or using MamaVoice Companion, you agree to dey bound by these Terms of Service. If you no agree to these terms, abeg no use our service. We reserve the right to update these terms anytime, and if you continue dey use the service, e mean say you accept any changes.
terms.s2body: MamaVoice na for adults wey reach 18 years and above who dey pregnant, new mamas, or people wey dey take care of newborns. By using this service, you dey confirm say you reach 18 years old, you dey use the service for lawful health information purpose, and you dey reside for jurisdiction where this service dey legally accessible.
terms.s3body: You responsible for: keeping your account credentials confidential; all activities wey happen under your account; giving accurate health information when asked; using MamaVoice as health information tool and not as replacement for professional medical care; and complying with all applicable laws. Notify us immediately if you find unauthorized use of your account at support{'@'}mamavoice.africa.
terms.s4body: You fit only use MamaVoice for lawful personal health information purposes. You agree no to: use the service to harm others; try to gain unauthorized access to our systems; use automated bots or scrapers; reproduce or commercially exploit our content without permission; upload malicious code or viruses; impersonate other users or MamaVoice staff.
terms.s5body: IMPORTANT: MamaVoice dey use artificial intelligence to provide maternal health information for educational purpose only. This no be medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. AI-generated responses dey based on general health guidelines and may not apply to your specific situation. Always talk to qualified doctor, midwife, or healthcare professional before you make medical decisions.
terms.s6body: ⚠️ MamaVoice NO BE emergency medical service. If you get medical emergency — including heavy bleeding, sudden severe headache or blurred vision, signs of preeclampsia, difficulty breathing, loss of consciousness, reduced fetal movement, severe abdominal pain, or any life-threatening condition — CALL 112 immediately or go to your nearest hospital. No dey rely on MamaVoice for emergency guidance.
terms.s7body: All content, features, and functionality of MamaVoice — including text, graphics, logos, software — na the exclusive property of MamaVoice Technologies Ltd, protected by Nigerian and international intellectual property laws. You no fit reproduce, distribute, or commercially exploit any MamaVoice content without our express written permission.
terms.s8body: By submitting content to MamaVoice — including questions, feedback, health data, and profile information — you dey give MamaVoice non-exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide licence to use, store, and process that content only to provide and improve our service. We no go ever identify you personally in any publicly shared content.
terms.s9body: To the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, MamaVoice Technologies Ltd no go be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including loss of profits, data, health outcomes, or other losses resulting from your use of the service; any AI-generated health information; unauthorized access to your data; or any other matter relating to the service.
terms.s10body: MamaVoice dey try to maintain high service availability. But we no guarantee say the service go dey available at all times without interruption or error. We fit suspend access for maintenance, security updates, or circumstances wey dey outside our control. Always get alternative contact with your healthcare provider for critical health needs.
terms.s11body: You fit terminate your MamaVoice account anytime through the app settings or by contacting support{'@'}mamavoice.africa. After termination, your account data go delete within 30 days. MamaVoice reserve the right to suspend or terminate your account immediately without notice if you violate these Terms.
terms.s12body: These Terms of Service go be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Federal Republic of Nigeria. Any disputes go first try resolve through good-faith negotiation. If negotiation fail, disputes go submit to binding arbitration in Lagos, Nigeria.
terms.s13body: For questions about these Terms: Email: legal{'@'}mamavoice.africa {'|'} Phone: +234 800 MAMA VOICE {'|'} MamaVoice Technologies Ltd, Lagos, Nigeria. Technical support: support{'@'}mamavoice.africa. We aim respond within 5 business days.

faq.sub: Everything wey you need to know before you start use MamaVoice Companion.
faq.openSourceDesc: MamaVoice web app na open source — developers and health organisations fit check am, add to am, and help improve di platform for every community.
faq.a0: MamaVoice Companion na voice-first AI health platform wey dem make for African mamas. E dey give trusted guide for pregnancy, newborn care, food, and vaccines — for Yorùbá, Hausa, Igbo, English, and Naija.
faq.q1: How di voice assistant dey work?
faq.a1: Just press di mic and talk your language. MamaVoice go use sharp AI understand your question and answer you with good health guide quick quick.
faq.a2: MamaVoice dey support English, Yorùbá, Hausa, Igbo, and Nigerian Pidgin (Naija). We dey always add more languages to reach more communities for Africa.
faq.q3: I fit ask question for Yorùbá, Hausa, Igbo, Pidgin, or English?
faq.a3: Yes o! You fit ask question for any language wey dem support. MamaVoice AI understand natural talk for all five languages and e go answer you for di same language wey you use.
faq.a4: Yes! Di web app and main features na free complete. Premium features like plenty specialist consultations and better health tracking dey available for affordable plans.
faq.a5: Absolutely. We dey use strong encryption to protect your health data, we no dey sell am to anybody, and we dey store am safely under di right data protection laws.
faq.q6: MamaVoice go replace my doctor or midwife?
faq.a6: No. MamaVoice dey give good health education and info, but e no be replacement for professional medical care. Always see your doctor, midwife, or health worker for your specific needs.
faq.q7: I fit track my pregnancy and vaccines?
faq.a7: Yes! MamaVoice get full health dashboard to track pregnancy progress, blood pressure, weight, baby kicks, and complete vaccine schedule for mama and baby.
faq.q8: Di platform dey work for phone and laptop?
faq.a8: Yes! MamaVoice dey work smooth on any smartphone, tablet, or computer through your browser. Dedicated mobile app for iOS and Android dey also available.
faq.q9: Doctors don review di AI guide?
faq.a9: Our AI don train on evidence-based maternal health guidelines from WHO, FMOH Nigeria, and big medical journals. E dey give reliable education but e no replace professional medical advice.
faq.q10: Community health workers fit use MamaVoice too?
faq.a10: Absolutely! Dem make MamaVoice to support community health workers, nurses, and midwives too. E dey work as a reliable tool during home visits, antenatal sessions, and community outreach.
faq.q11: How I go switch between languages?
faq.a11: You fit switch language anytime using di language selector for di nav bar or footer. Your choice go save automatically and apply everywhere for di platform.

contact.metaDescription: Get in touch with MamaVoice. We dey here to help you with any questions about our maternal health platform.
contact.heroSub: You get question, feedback, or need support? Our team dey ready to help.
contact.formMessagePlaceholder: Tell us how we fit help you…
contact.formSuccess: Thank you! We don receive your message. We go reply within 24 hours.
contact.formError: Something go wrong. Abeg try again or email us directly at hello{'@'}mamavoice.africa.
contact.errNameMin: Name must be at least 2 characters.
contact.errMessageMin: Message must be at least 10 characters.
contact.emergencyText: MamaVoice no be emergency service. If you dey experience medical emergency, abeg call 112 immediately or go to your nearest hospital.
contact.faq0a: Yes! The MamaVoice web app and basic features na completely free. Premium features like unlimited specialist consultations dey available through our affordable subscription plans.
contact.faq1a: MamaVoice support English, Yorùbá, Hausa, Igbo, and Naija Pidgin. Our AI dey understand and respond in whichever language you prefer.
contact.faq2q: How accurate the AI health guidance be?
contact.faq2a: We train our AI on evidence-based maternal health guidelines from WHO, FMOH Nigeria, and leading medical journals. While e dey give reliable general guidance, e no be substitute for professional medical advice. Always talk to your doctor or midwife for personalised care.
contact.faq3q: My health data safe and private?
contact.faq3a: Absolutely. We dey take data privacy serious. Your health information dey encrypted, we never sell am to third parties, and we only use am to provide your personalised health guidance.
contact.faq4q: I fit use MamaVoice without internet?
contact.faq4a: The web app need internet connection. The mobile app (coming soon) go include offline support for essential features including saved health records and vaccination schedules.
contact.faq5q: How I go delete my account and data?
contact.faq5a: You fit request account deletion anytime by emailing privacy{'@'}mamavoice.africa. All your personal data go permanently delete within 30 days of your request.
contact.communitySub: Connect with thousands of mamas for our WhatsApp support community for tips, encouragement, and shared experiences.
